front: Author: 18th-century Bristol poet who forged poems attributed to the fictional 15th-century monk Thomas Rowley; committed suicide at age 17
Author: called a "marvellous Boy" in Wordsworth's "Resolution and Independence"
Author: dedicatee of Keats's Endymion ("A thing of beauty is a joy forever")
Author: death scene painted by Henry Wallis using George Meredith as model; the painting hangs in the Tate
Author: named alongside Sidney and Lucan as "inheritors of unfulfill'd renown" in Shelley's Adonais
Author: Robert Browning adopted the term "slug-horn" from this poet's pseudo-medieval language
Poem: "the budding floweret blushes at the light" opens the Minstrel's Marriage-Song in this Rowley poem
